name: Lamboya

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Lamboya (lamb1273).

notes: Skeleton. Lamboya is a Central Malayo-Polynesian (Sumba-Hawu) Austronesian language of western Sumba, Indonesia, written in a Latin orthography of the Indonesian type. The grapheme map covers the five-vowel system and a basic consonant inventory including the digraphs ng and ny. Sumba languages typically contrast implosive stops (bilabial and alveolar) with plain stops; where the orthography does not mark implosion, that contrast is not encoded here. Prenasalization, stress, and vowel length are also left unencoded. This is a low-confidence skeleton drawn from classificatory sources; a dedicated phonological description would upgrade it.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Lamboya (lamb1273)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
